CommissioIl, 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 
(916) 322·5662 
Tony Quinn 
Policy Director 
Administration •• 
Executive/legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
322.5660 
322·5901 
322-6-441 
December 19, 1983 
Assembly Republican Caucus 
1100 J Street 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Advice Letter A-83-277 
Dear Tony: 
You have asked whether Government Code Section 84309 
prohibits receipt of campaign contributions by a member of the 
Legislature in his or her district office if: 
(1) 
The district office is located in a state office 
building; Or 
(2) 
The district office is located .in a building for which 
the sta~e pays the majority of the rent. 
Section 84309(a) provides: 
No person shall receive or personally deliver or 
attempt to deliver a contribution in the State Capitol, 
in any state office building, or in any office for 
which the state pays the majority of the rent other 
than a legislative district office. 
(Emphasis added.) 
The intent of the underlined language was to exempt all 
district offices from the prohibition against delivery of 
campaign contributions. 
Therefore a member of the Legislature 
may accept campaign contributions in his or her district office, 
even if the office is located in a state office building or in a 
building for which the state pays the majority of the rent. 
/ 
BAM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Barbara A. Milman 
General Counsel 


E 
Barbara Milman, Esq. 
Chief, Legal Division 
LY R 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Barbara: 
~ 
Government Code Section 84309 prohibits the receipt of a 
campaign contribution delivered in person at the State Capitol or 
at any state office building. 
Such prohibition extends to 
receipt at any office for which the state pays the majority of 
the rent with the exception of legislative district offices. 
This section also prohibits the delivery or the attempted 
delivery of a contribution made in person or by an agent or 
intermediary. 
I have the following questions: 
Maya member of the 
Legislature accept a campaign contribution in his or her district 
office if that office is located in a state office building. 
May 
a member accept a contribution if the district office is located 
in a building for which the state pays the majority of the rent. 
Thank you for your prompt response to these questions. 
Sincerely, 
~" 
Tony Quinn 
Policy Director 
TQ:nw 
Sa,.;::.un~ntc. CA

sections.question: You have asked whether Government Code Section 84309 prohibits receipt of campaign contributions by a member of the Legislature in his or her district office if: (1) The district office is located in a state office building; Or (2) The district office is located in a building for which the state pays the majority of the rent.
sections.conclusion: The intent of the underlined language was to exempt all district offices from the prohibition against delivery of campaign contributions. Therefore a member of the Legislature may accept campaign contributions in his or her district office, even if the office is located in a state office building or in a building for which the state pays the majority of the rent.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Government Code Section 84309 prohibit legislators from accepting campaign contributions in their district offices when those offices are located in state office buildings or buildings where the state pays the majority of rent?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No, Section 84309 does not prohibit receipt of campaign contributions in legislative district offices. The statute explicitly exempts all district offices from the contribution delivery prohibition, regardless of whether they are located in state office buildings or buildings where the state pays the majority of rent.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a formal advice letter with both incoming request and FPPC response included. The question and conclusion sections are clearly delineated. The document is well-structured and contains explicit statutory analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked whether Government Code Section 84309 prohibits receipt of campaign contributions by a member of the Legislature in his or her district office if: (1) The district office is located in a state office building; Or (2) The district office is located in a building for which the state pays the majority of the rent.

CONCLUSION: The intent of the underlined language was to exempt all district offices from the prohibition against delivery of campaign contributions. Therefore a member of the Legislature may accept campaign contributions in his or her district office, even if the office is located in a state office building or in a building for which the state pays the majority of the rent.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Barbara Milman advises that Government Code Section 84309 exempts legislative district offices from the prohibition on accepting campaign contributions, even when those offices are located in state buildings or buildings where the state pays the majority of rent.
